subject: Science
description: describe the experimental development of atomic theory using the historical models of Democritus, Thomson, Rutherford, and Bohr

subject: Science
description: describe the mathematical relationships between the number and arrangement of atoms in chemical formulas and compound names

subject: Science
description: calculate the atomic mass of an element using the natural abundance and mass of its isotopes

subject: Science
description: understand the use of electron configuration and valence electrons to determine an element's placement on the periodic table

subject: Science
description: identify and explain the trend of electronegativity, ionization energy, and atomic radius using the periodic table

subject: Science
description: describe the unique role of water in chemical and biological systems

subject: Science
description: identify and describe physical and chemical properties of elements and compounds, including color, density, viscosity, buoyancy, boiling point, freezing point, conductivity, and reactivity

subject: Science
description: compare and contrast the physical and chemical properties of mixtures and pure substances

subject: Science
description: describe chemical bonding using electron dot structures, including ionic bonds, covalent bonds, metallic bonds, and van der Waals forces

subject: Science
description: draw Lewis structures to explain the bonding in simple molecules and polyatomic ions

subject: Science
description: describe the law of conservation of mass and demonstrate it through balancing chemical equations

subject: Science
description: identify and classify chemical reaction types, including synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, and double replacement reactions

subject: Science
description: describe the concept of the mole and use it in calculations involving mass, moles, and number of molecules

subject: Science
description: define and use the concept of stoichiometry including calculations of quantities of reactants or products in chemical equations

subject: Science
description: identify and describe factors that influence the rate of chemical reactions, including temperature, concentration, surface area, and catalysts

subject: Science
description: describe energy changes in exothermic and endothermic chemical reactions using calorimetry and calculate enthalpy changes

subject: Science
description: describe the process of dissolving, including the roles of the solute and solvent, and factors that affect solubility such as temperature and pressure

subject: Science
description: calculate the concentration of solutions in units of molarity

subject: Science
description: define acids and bases and distinguish between Arrhenius and Bronsted-Lowry definitions and predict products of acid-base reactions
